attacking_style: No longer initiating attacks; attempting to follow decisive moves in final 15-40km range but unable to sustain efforts in the critical final hour of major Classics.
team_tactics: Quick-Step built numerical advantage (three riders forward at Ronde) to compensate for diminished individual capability, but mechanicals and execution failures undermined strategy.
terrain_preference: Historically suited to cobbled Classics (Paris-Roubaix, Ronde van Vlaanderen), but 2017 observations show declining ability to handle decisive terrain in race-winning moments regardless of surface type.
vulnerability: Critical weakness in final hour execution: unable to follow moves at 15km (Paris-Roubaix), positioning errors in sprint finishes (Oman crash), and mechanical misfortune at decisive moments (Ronde chain jam).
key_patterns: Present with leaders at 40km+ but fading before finish, suggesting declining endurance. (2 races)
Execution failures in decisive moments: mechanical issues, positioning errors, inability to follow attacks. (3 races)
Team building numerical advantages to support him, indicating compensatory tactics for reduced form. (1 race)
form_trajectory: negative: consistent pattern of being competitive mid-race but failing to execute in final decisive phases across all three 2017 observations, suggesting age-related decline
